member.name: Ben Goldsborough
member.synopsis: Ben Goldsborough is the Labour MP for South Norfolk , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Recreation Spaces
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what assessment he has made of the adequacy of access to high-quality green spaces across the UK.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The government is committed to ensuring everyone lives within a 15-minute walk of a green or blue space. In response to this commitment, earlier this year Defra published official statistics in development showing that 80% of people are within this range,...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Video Games: Curriculum
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what steps she is taking to ensure the curriculum is encouraging young people to consider careers in the video games sector.
writtenQuestions.answerText: To ensure that children and young people are equipped with the essential knowledge and skills required for the future, this government is taking decisive action to modernise digital education.Following the final report of the independent Curriculum and As...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education

writtenQuestions.heading: Property: Income
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, pursuant to the Answer of 15 June 2026 to Question 7262, what proportion of taxpayers reporting a gross property income greater than £1,000 claimed property allowance, and at what cost to the treasury.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The proportion of taxpayers reporting a gross property income greater than £1,000 that claimed property allowance in the latest available financial year (2023/24) is estimated to be 8%. An estimate of the Exchequer cost is not readily available as Landlor...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Treasury

writtenQuestions.heading: Video Games: Work Experience
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what steps she is taking to help expand access to work experience placements for young people interested in careers in the video games industry.
writtenQuestions.answerText: This government is working with industry to deliver a UK-wide £9 million creative careers initiative, to equip young people with the ambition and knowledge to work in the creative industries. This includes ‘Into Games’ workshops in schools led by trained ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education

writtenQuestions.heading: Video Games: Tax Allowances
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, if she will make an assessment of the potential merits of increasing the proportion of qualifying expenditure for Video Games Expenditure Credit (VGEC) from 80 per cent to 100 per cent.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government recognises the importance of the creative industries, including the contribution made by the UK’s video games sector to growth and innovation. Video games companies already benefit from a generous Video Games Expenditure Credit (VGEC) of 34...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Treasury

writtenQuestions.heading: Video Games
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, whether her department will review the effectiveness of the updated Standard Industrial Classification codes for the video games industry following their implementation.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The new 2026 UK Standard Industrial Classification (SIC) framework will introduce a new dedicated 4-digit code for video games development, alongside the existing 4-digit code for video games publishing and new activity such as esports. My department will...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.answerText: This Government has committed to supporting the video games sector as part of the Creative Industries Sector Plan, published last year, which sets out support for the industry in order to address the challenges it faces. In April, we launched the £30 mill...

writtenQuestions.heading: Video Games: Finance

writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government has reaffirmed its support for SME growth and the startup ecosystem, extending both the Enterprise Investment Scheme and Venture Capital Trust scheme to 2035. Our 10-year Industrial Strategy and Creative Industries Sector Plan set out how w...

writtenQuestions.heading: Hong Kong: Human Rights
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, what recent assessment she has made of the potential implications for her Department's policies of the human rights situation in Hong Kong.
writtenQuestions.answerText: On 26 March, the Foreign Secretary published the Government’s latest six-monthly report on Hong Kong, which repeated our concerns about the continued erosion of rights and freedoms in Hong Kong, in breach of the Sino-British Joint Declaration.Ministers an...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Genetically Modified Organisms: Plants
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, pursuant to the Answers of 27 May 2026 to Questions 2585 and 2586, on what date will a National Listing system recognising precision bred seeds be available.
writtenQuestions.answerText: On 9 March, this Government published the legislation expected to be in scope of the Sanitary and Phytosanitary (SPS) Agreement currently being negotiated with the EU. This includes legislation relating to plant varieties and seeds. As negotiations are on...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Social Security Benefits: Employment
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, what is the annual cost of paying legacy benefits such as working tax credits and earlier iterations of Universal Credit to people in paid employment.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Precursors to Universal Credit (UC) such as Working Tax Credits (WTC) have now migrated to UC, therefore legacy payments are no longer applicable. As a result, all associated costs are captured within UC payments. For other working age benefits where clai...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Work and Pensions

writtenQuestions.heading: Universal Credit: Employment
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, what is the annual cost of paying Universal Credit to people in paid employment.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The department publishes figures for expenditure on Universal Credit (UC) Expenditure here: Benefit expenditure and caseload tables - GOV.UK Universal Credit entitlement is calculated on a household basis. The requested breakdown of UC expenditure to peop...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Work and Pensions

writtenQuestions.heading: Universal Credit: Employment

writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Work and Pensions

items.text: I was elected as an Officer of the Labour Rural Research Group (LRRG), a formally constituted group within the Labour Party focused on rural policy development and advocacy. This is an unpaid role. Date interest arose: 26 January 2026 (Registered 19 June 2026)
